statement.en: Among 25 'atomic cores' (small-ord_p(3) primes concentrated as factors in Collatz peak sequences), prime 13 appears 4/25 = 16% vs natural density 7.69%, a 2.08x enrichment. Similarly, small primes with low ord_p(3) (5, 7, 11, 13) are over-represented. Conjecture: small-ord_p(3) primes have a structural role in Collatz orbit ascents, specifically in peak-9232 neighborhood and similar excursion regimes.
statement.ja: 25 atomic cores で素数 13 が 2.08x 濃縮 (4/25 = 16% vs 期待 7.69%). 小 ord_p(3) 素数が構造的役割. 予想: 低い ord_p(3) 素数が Collatz orbit 上昇挙動の構造的中核

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: 数値統計と構造の対応
reiTyping.rationale: 2.08x enrichment is statistically significant beyond random chance

formalization.notes: Statistical claim via direct enumeration of atomic core primes

known_progress.event: Partial counterexample: n=577 (peak-9232 factor) has ord_577(3) = 48, NOT small

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Open: proving 2.08x enrichment is asymptotic or finite-sample artifact
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Requires density arguments over all primes with given ord_p(3)
